findings.description: Source contains a tip block explicitly stating that non-framework content (documents, development standards, tools) is excluded from the backward compatibility policy: "フレームワーク以外のコンテンツは後方互換性の維持の対象外です。" with rationale explaining why (old version docs are accessible from old version documentation). This information is absent from the knowledge file. An AI answering questions like "does Nablarch maintain backward compatibility for its documentation or development standards?" would lack the basis to give a correct answer.
findings.source_evidence: Source tip block under 後方互換性を維持する範囲: "フレームワーク以外のコンテンツは後方互換性の維持の対象外です。" followed by explanation about why docs/tools/standards don't need backward compat guarantees.
